data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but the file is never added to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild SDK collects `Compile` items before `CoreCompile` runs, so a file written to the intermediate output path won't be automatically included.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />`. This is the standard pattern for source generators in MSBuild.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <!-- Include the generated file in the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>"}

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes a `.cs` file to the intermediate output directory, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. Files in `obj/` aren't auto-included by the SDK, so the compiler never sees the generated source.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation after it's written.
